THE DESIGNER IS WORKING ON A POSTER WITH EIGHT CONCENTRIC CIRCLES. EACH CIRCLE HAS A RADIUS THAT IS 10% LARGER THAN THE PREVIOUS ONE. THE TOTAL AREA COVERED BY ALL EIGHT CIRCLES CAN BE CALCULATED USING THE FORMULA FOR THE AREA OF A CIRCLE, WHICH IS πR². THE FIRST CIRCLE HAS A RADIUS OF R, THE SECOND HAS A RADIUS OF 1.1R, THE THIRD HAS A RADIUS OF (1.1)²R, AND SO ON, UNTIL THE EIGHTH CIRCLE, WHICH HAS A RADIUS OF (1.1)⁷R. THE TOTAL AREA IS THE SUM OF THE AREAS OF ALL EIGHT CIRCLES. THIS SUM CAN BE EXPRESSED AS πR²(1 + (1.1)² + (1.1)⁴ + (1.1)⁶ + ... + (1.1)¹⁴). THIS GEOMETRIC SERIES HAS A COMMON RATIO OF (1.1)², AND THE SUM CAN BE CALCULATED USING THE FORMULA FOR THE SUM OF A GEOMETRIC SERIES. THE TOTAL AREA IS THEREFORE πR² MULTIPLIED BY THE SUM OF THE SERIES. THIS GIVES THE TOTAL AREA COVERED BY ALL EIGHT CIRCLES.

FOR THE FIFTH CIRCLE, THE RADIUS IS (1.1)⁴R. THE CIRCUMFERENCE OF THIS CIRCLE IS 2π(1.1)⁴R. THIS CIRCUMFERENCE IS TO BE USED TO PLACE TEXT CHARACTERS WITH A 0.5 CM GAP BETWEEN EACH CHARACTER. TO DETERMINE THE MAXIMUM NUMBER OF CHARACTERS THAT CAN BE PLACED, WE DIVIDE THE CIRCUMFERENCE BY THE TOTAL SPACE REQUIRED PER CHARACTER, INCLUDING THE GAP. THIS SPACE IS THE SUM OF THE WIDTH OF THE CHARACTER AND THE GAP. HOWEVER, SINCE THE TOTAL NUMBER OF CHARACTERS MUST BE AN INTEGER, WE MUST FIND THE LARGEST INTEGER THAT FITS INTO THE CIRCUMFERENCE DIVIDED BY THE SPACE PER CHARACTER. THE CIRCUMFERENCE IS 2π(1.1)⁴R. THIS VALUE MUST BE DIVIDED BY 0.5 TO DETERMINE HOW MANY 0.5 CM SEGMENTS FIT INTO THE CIRCUMFERENCE. THE RESULT MUST BE
